scenario: Aurora Retail uses a DMN table in Camunda 8.8 to pick a single advertisement banner for each visitor. The table contains 6 overlapping age-based rules, and the team wants only the top rule in display order to win when several rules match a 19 year old customer browsing 250 products per session.
question: Which hit policy delivers exactly one output equal to the topmost satisfied rule's output entry?

options.text: FIRST hit policy returns only the output of the first satisfied rule according to the rule ordering in the decision table
UNIQUE hit policy returns at most one output but raises an evaluation error if more than one of the 6 rules matches
RULE ORDER hit policy still returns every satisfied rule, just in the order in which the rules appear in the table
ANY hit policy returns one output, but only if all 6 rules that match produce the same exact output entry

a.text: Correct. FIRST takes only the first satisfied rule's output by rule order, which is exactly what the team wants for the banner choice.

b.text: Incorrect. UNIQUE forbids multiple matches; the scenario explicitly expects overlapping rules and would raise an evaluation error.

c.text: Incorrect. RULE ORDER returns a list of all satisfied rules' outputs, not the single first one.

d.text: Incorrect. ANY needs identical outputs across satisfied rules, which different banner choices do not provide.

explanation: FIRST is the documented Camunda DMN hit policy that returns the output of the first satisfied rule by row order, which fits the single-banner selection use case.
